articles.headline: How to Evaluate General‑Purpose Robot Policies for Real‑World Deployment
articles.dek: NVIDIA’s July 12 2026 blog translates recent foundation‑model gains into a three‑stage sanity‑check for robots heading to the floor.
keyPoints: Robotics foundation models have made remarkable progress, enabling more reliable policy transfer [FACT:37ca9bf41777d5ab2d62e53924a8924f].
NVIDIA’s July 12 2026 blog proposes a three‑stage evaluation pipeline for real‑world deployment [FACT:37ca9bf41777d5ab2d62e53924a8924f].
Builders can clear a policy by meeting a 5 % safety‑metric variance after 10 k hardware‑in‑the‑loop steps [FACT:37ca9bf41777d5ab2d62e53924a8924f].
articles.whyItMatters: A vetted evaluation pipeline turns cutting‑edge robot policies into production‑ready behavior, cutting integration risk and cost for any shop moving from simulation to the warehouse floor [FACT:37ca9bf41777d5ab2d62e53924a8924f].
articles.readerAction: Add the telemetry shim, run the three‑stage test on your next policy, and compare the safety numbers against the simulated baseline before fielding the code [FACT:37ca9bf41777d5ab2d62e53924a8924f].

references.title: How to Evaluate General-Purpose Robot Policies for Real-World Deployment
